post.title: Provenance Before Persuasion
post.intent_statement: Insist that generated text must be visibly distinct from authored prose, always.
post.central_question: What makes a generated sentence trustworthy?
post.core_insight: Trust comes from seeing where a thought came from, not from how smooth it reads.
post.body_html:
Generated text earns its place only when it cannot be mistaken for the author.

Serif is the author; mono is the system. The seam is a feature, not a flaw.

Provenance that is only metadata is not trust. The reader has to be able to see the source.
